title: I Built a Photoshop AI Agent in n8n with no code (NanoBanana)

extracted.hook: Today I'm super excited to share with you guys this Photoshop AI agent that I built in Nen with no code.
transitions: All right. So here is the master Photoshop agent.
Okay, so you can see that our workflow is listening to us.

And then for the instructions, I only ended up giving it one instruction, which was if the user submits a photo, ask what they want to name it by saying, "What would you like me to name that photo in your Google Drive?"
Okay, so shooting off this message that says to call that picture granola.
All right, so it just told us it was done.
extracted.cta: "if you enjoyed or you learned something new, please give it a like. It definitely helps me out a ton."
extracted.analogies: "it's basically using its combined image tool, and it's going to use nano banana to make it look like I'm holding the granola on a mountain"
curiosity_gaps: "And then real quick, just to hit on what's on over here before we dive into the tools is we're using GBT 5.1"
"So that's how we can create these really modular systems because now if I ever want to create another agent that can combine images, I can just plug it into this workflow right here"
"And then once it gets that result back, it basically gives us a URL"
"Real quick, just wanted to touch on some of the pricing for this system."
extracted.vulnerability: "two things, the first thing is that in the RAW files, this is a pretty low quality image. So, like I can't even read what that says right there"

slows: explaining the system prompt in detail
